hypertension.description: Doctor visit with elevated numbers → dietary changes (cutting sodium, meal prep) → follow-up showing improvement

shellfish.description: Avoiding shared fryers at parish fish fry → bringing own food to Lent events → bad restaurant experience referenced when picking new restaurant

career.description: Frustration about Kaylee's scheduling power → considering ServSafe cert for new location → researching community college hospitality program

messenger_logs.relationship: Mother — closest family member, sees 2x/week, shared cooking tradition and shellfish sensitivity, devout Catholic who attends Sunday Mass every week

embedded_facts: Shellfish sensitivity (shared with mom) — Mary bringing her own dish to parish fish fry to avoid cross-contamination from shared fryers
Catholic faith — parish fish fry volunteering, Lent rhythm
Mom is protective about food safety, 'always ask twice' refrain

messages.text: Mary are you still helping at the fish fry this Friday? Father Tom asked about volunteers for cleanup.
yes!! i'll be there right after my shift so probably like 7:30ish
Good. I'm making the baked cod and the calamari for the table. Do you want me to set a plate aside for you with just the cod?
yes please lol you know the drill. i'm gonna bring my own pasta salad too just in case. last time somebody used the same fryer for everything and i was NOT about to risk it
Smart girl. Always ask twice. I'll make sure nobody puts anything near your plate. Love Mom

embedded_facts: Hypertension — doctor visit with elevated numbers, told to reduce sodium and recheck
Family history — Nonna has cardiac issues
Mom is worried and protective, immediately offers practical help (cookbook)

messages.text: hey mom just got out of my checkup
And?? How did it go?
fine i guess. numbers were still a little high. she wants me to really watch the sodium and come back in a few months
Mary. You need to take this seriously. You know Nonna has had problems with all of that. Did the doctor say anything about medication?
no no it's not that bad. she said keep running and cut back on salt and recheck in like 6 months. i'm fine mom i promise
I am going to send you that low sodium cookbook I got from the library. You are eating too much of that fast food at work. I know you are.
lol ok ok i'll look at it. love you
Love you more. Take care of yourself please. Love Mom

embedded_facts: Sunday dinner tradition — Italian-American family, homemade rigatoni
Nonna slowing down but still going to daily Mass
Lent starting — fish Fridays, Catholic observance
Mom coordinates family logistics

messages.text: Are you coming for dinner Sunday? Nonna wants to make her rigatoni but I told her I'd help this time. She's been tired.
oh no is she ok??
She's fine Mary. Just slowing down a little. She went to daily Mass this morning so she can't be that tired. But I want to do the heavy lifting so she can sit and tell me I'm doing it wrong.
lmaooo that is literally her favorite activity. ok yes i'll be there. also reminder lent starts wednesday so are we doing fish fridays at your place or mine
Mine. I already bought halibut for this Friday. No shellfish obviously. Bring a salad if you want. Love Mom

embedded_facts: Hypertension improving — numbers better after dietary changes and continued running
Mom's prayers and recipes contributing to Mary's motivation
Dad also doesn't listen about sodium (family dynamic)

messages.text: MOM guess what
What?? Are you OK??
lol yes calm down. i checked my numbers at the walgreens machine and they were actually way better than january!! the running and cutting back on salt is actually working
Oh thank God. See??? I told you. Your father never listens to me about the salt either but at least one of my children has some sense. Keep it up Mary.
hahaha poor dad. ok i gotta go back in my break is almost over. see you sunday?
Yes. I'm making lentil soup. Low sodium. Don't argue with me. Love Mom

messenger_logs.relationship: Running club friend — closest non-family friend, ~10 years older, half-marathon runner, accountability partner, mentor figure who pushes Mary in running and life planning

embedded_facts: Running routine — Lake Mendota, Madison Runners Club, MWF mornings
Dana as accountability partner and experienced runner
Financial constraint — Mary checks budget before committing to race entry fee

messages.text: Happy new year! Are you running tomorrow morning? Supposed to be 12 degrees but no wind
oh for sure i'm going. new year new me and all that lol. i already set out my layers
That's what I like to hear! I want to push for 4.5 miles. My half marathon training officially starts this month and I need to build my base back up after the holidays
wait you're doing ANOTHER half??? ok queen. i'll try to keep up
You should seriously think about signing up too! The Madison Mini is in May. It would be your first half but you are absolutely ready for it
mmm the entry fee tho. let me check my budget. you know i have a whole spreadsheet for this lol
Ha! Your bullet journal empire. Let me know. It would be fun to train together!

embedded_facts: Dana texts when Mary misses a run — accountability dynamic
Mary is routine-dependent, gets anxious when schedule is disrupted
Lake Mendota morning run pattern

messages.text: Didn't see you at the lake this morning! Everything ok?
ugh i know i'm sorry. my alarm didn't go off and then i had to be at work by 9 for a shift change. my whole morning was thrown off and honestly my brain has been weird all day because of it
Ha I totally get it. You're the most routine-dependent person I know and I mean that as a compliment. Friday for sure though right?
friday for SURE. i need it. this week has been A LOT

embedded_facts: Hypertension — dietary changes in progress, cutting sodium, meal prepping more
Dana's mom had similar health situation — normalizes the experience
Financial constraint — eating at work to save money but it conflicts with health goals
Running linked to health management

messages.text: ok random question do you have any good low sodium recipes? i've been trying to cut back and i'm running out of ideas. everything i usually make has like cheese and salt and more cheese

Omg yes! I went through a whole phase of that when my mom was dealing with similar stuff. I'll text you a few links tonight. Have you tried roasting vegetables with just lemon and herbs? Total game changer
ooh ok yeah send them please. i've been meal prepping more because if i don't i just eat fries at work all day and that is NOT helping my situation
Ugh the work food trap. I remember that from my waitressing days. Prep is everything. Also those morning runs are doing more for you than you realize. Keep showing up!
you're literally my coach AND my therapist lol thank you

embedded_facts: Career advancement — Mary researched community college hospitality program and ServSafe certification
Financial constraint — $200 is significant, needs extra shifts, has it in budget journal
Dana as mentor — encourages FAFSA, has been pushing SBA loan research
Diner dream is a long-term goal Mary is actively working toward

messages.text: so i actually looked into that community college hospitality program you mentioned. they have a spring enrollment window for the ServSafe cert and it's only like $200
YES!! Mary!! That is amazing. Did you look at financial aid too? You should absolutely apply for FAFSA
ugh i know i keep putting that off. paperwork makes my brain melt. but yeah $200 is doable if i pick up an extra shift or two. i already have it penciled into my budget journal
You and that bullet journal honestly run the world. Have you thought any more about the SBA loan stuff? For down the road with the diner?
one thing at a time lol. ServSafe first then world domination. but yeah i bookmarked the SBA site so baby steps
Baby steps that are going somewhere! I'm proud of you. See you at 6:15 Friday?
wouldn't miss it

messenger_logs.relationship: Coworker at the same fast food restaurant — trusted confidante, shift-swap partner, the one person Mary can vent to at work, Brewers game buddy

embedded_facts: Career frustration — Kaylee got promoted over Mary and now controls scheduling
Mary has more seniority but no title
Catholic practice — Mary can't close Friday because of parish fish fry
Shift-swap dynamic between Mary and Jess
Farmers market Saturday routine

messages.text: dude kaylee just made the new schedule and she put us both on close friday AND saturday. i literally cannot
are you KIDDING me. i specifically told her i can't do friday close because of the fish fry thing at church. she KNOWS that
she doesn't care. ever since she got that title she's been on a whole power trip
don't even get me started. i trained her jess. i've been here 2 years longer. and now she's making MY schedule?? make it make sense
it doesn't. literally everyone knows you should've gotten it. can you swap with me at least? i'll take your friday if you take my saturday day shift
yeah that works actually. saturday day means i can still hit the farmers market in the morning. you're a lifesaver jess for real
that's what we do

embedded_facts: Shellfish sensitivity — bad restaurant experience referenced ('stomach was NOT happy')
Mary always double-checks menus, calls ahead about shared fryers
Financial constraint — Mary is on a budget for eating out

messages.text: hey are you still down for that new Thai place on state street saturday?
yesss i've been wanting to try it forever. 6:30 work?
perfect. also can you check if their menu is online? i need to look at it before we go. you know how i am lol
lol yes i know. gotta investigate every ingredient. i'll send you the link
listen after that pasta incident last year where my stomach was NOT happy for like two days straight i am not taking any chances anymore. i'm gonna call ahead and ask about shared fryers too
totally fair. honestly i'll eat whatever you can eat i really don't care. it's the company that matters

embedded_facts: Career advancement — Mary considering ServSafe certification
New restaurant location opening in Sun Prairie needs supervisors
Financial constraint — $200 is a lot right now, framing it as investment
Kaylee conflict referenced indirectly — 'NOT getting passed over again'
Channeling frustration into self-improvement

messages.text: ok so random but have you ever thought about getting your ServSafe? like the actual certification
no lol why would i voluntarily take a test. aren't we done with school
lmaoo ok fair. but i'm seriously thinking about it. like if i get certified it looks way better when i apply for the supervisor spot at the new location they're opening
wait there's a new location?? where
sun prairie. derek mentioned it last week. they're gonna need supervisors and i am NOT getting passed over again
ok yeah that's actually really smart. get the cert before anyone else even thinks about it. how much is it
like $200 for the course and exam. which is a lot for me rn but it's an investment right? that's what i keep telling myself
it IS an investment. and you'd be an amazing supervisor honestly. way better than... you know who
JESS lmaooo stop. but also... yeah. ok i'm doing it

embedded_facts: Brewers fandom — planning opening day trip to Milwaukee
Financial constraint — bringing snacks instead of buying at stadium, budget-conscious planning
Shift-swap dynamic, work schedule coordination
Farmers market cheese curds — Saturday routine

messages.text: OPENING DAY IS IN TWO WEEKS
I KNOW i've been counting down. are we doing the milwaukee trip this year or what
absolutely. i already asked off for that saturday. april 11th. let's gooo
ok let me see if i can swap that shift. we should get the cheap seats and just bring our own snacks. i'm trying not to spend like $80 at the stadium again lol
lol budget queen mary strikes again. deal. i'll drive if you bring the food
done. cheese curds from marcus at the farmers market and homemade trail mix. we're doing this RIGHT

messenger_logs.relationship: Grandmother — matriarch, Calabrian immigrant in her 80s, daily Mass attendee, origin of Mary's cooking tradition, not a heavy texter (Mary texts to coordinate visits, Nonna replies simply)

embedded_facts: Wednesday visit routine — Mary checks in on Nonna weekly
Nonna is in her 80s, texts sparingly and directly
Local Madison detail — Metcalfe's grocery store
Nonna still actively involved in directing family life

messages.text: Hi Nonna!! I'm coming over Wednesday after work ok? Should be there by 7:30. Do you need me to bring anything?
Yes come. Bring bread. The good kind from Metcalfes.
lol ok the good kind. got it. love you see you wednesday
Love you too. Dont forget.

embedded_facts: Shellfish sensitivity — Mary bringing her own pasta to Lent fish fry because she can't eat most of the dishes
Catholic Lent observance — parish fish fry tradition
Nonna accommodates Mary's sensitivity (uses only olive oil, no shared shrimp oil)
Nonna making baccala — traditional Italian salt cod dish
Three generations of women coordinating the family's religious food tradition

messages.text: Nonna are you making anything for the Lent fish fry at church this friday?
I am making the baccala. Your mother is doing the rest. You come help us.
i'll be there! i'm making my own pasta to bring too since i can't eat half the stuff there lol. you know me
I know. You are like your mother. I will make sure the baccala has no shrimp in the oil. Olive oil only.
you're the best nonna. seriously. see you friday

embedded_facts: Nonna's health — slowing down, Mary and Mom both monitoring her
Nonna still going to daily Mass despite being tired (devotion)
Garden season starting — Nonna's Calabrian knowledge about growing tomatoes
Wednesday visit routine continues
Easter timeline — tomato seedlings after Easter

messages.text: Hey Nonna! Coming Wednesday as usual. Mom said you were tired this week. Are you feeling ok?
I am fine. Your mother worries too much. I went to Mass every day this week.
lol of course you did. ok well rest up anyway. also!! it's almost garden season and i want to ask you about when to start the tomato seedlings. you always know the best time
After Easter. Not before. The frost will kill them. I will show you when you come.
ok deal. love you nonna see you wednesday

messenger_logs.relationship: Younger brother — community college student in Madison, Mary is the protective older sister, sees him ~1x/week mostly at Sunday dinner

embedded_facts: Sunday dinner tradition — Italian-American family gathering
Packers fandom — family bonding, Tony less motivated without a game on
Mom's rigatoni and Nonna attending
Tony is casual about family obligations, Mary keeps him in line

messages.text: you coming to dinner today or what
yeah probably. packers aren't on tho so whats the point lol
the POINT is mom made rigatoni and nonna is coming so get your butt over there
ok ok fine. what time
like 2. don't be late or mom will give you the look
lmao the look. say less i'll be there

embedded_facts: Tony in community college — Mary checking on his midterms
Mary as supportive older sister offering to help
Career frustration — Mary still waiting on supervisor promotion, confides briefly in Tony
Tony is supportive in his own low-key way

messages.text: hey how'd your midterms go
ehh. passed everything. econ is killing me tho
passing is passing!! do you need help with anything? i'm no good at econ but i can quiz you or whatever
nah i'm good. thanks tho. hows work
same old same old. still waiting on that supervisor thing. trying not to lose my mind about it lol
you'll get it. you're literally the hardest worker there
thanks tony. that actually means a lot coming from you

embedded_facts: Lent fish fry — Catholic family tradition, parish event
Mom volunteered Tony without asking — family dynamic
Mary bringing her own pasta salad (shellfish cross-contamination avoidance)
Tony's more casual relationship with church obligations

messages.text: are you coming to the fish fry at church tomorrow
do i have a choice
no lol mom already put your name down to help set up chairs
of course she did. fine. what time
5:30 setup, food starts at 6:30. i'm bringing pasta salad. just show up and be helpful for once
WOW ok. i am helpful. sometimes. see you there

embedded_facts: Career advancement — Mary researching community college hospitality program herself
Tony attends Madison College, Mary wants his perspective
ServSafe certification tied to supervisor ambitions
Diner dream — Mary's long-term goal, self-aware about needing more than 'vibes and a bullet journal'
Sibling bond — Tony is genuinely excited for Mary, offers to show her around campus

messages.text: ok so weird question. how do you like madison college? like the actual campus and the classes and stuff
its fine? why lol
i'm looking at their hospitality program. thinking about taking some classes. maybe evenings or weekends around my work shifts
wait seriously?? thats sick mary. you should totally do it
yeah i mean if i ever want to actually open the diner i need more than just vibes and a bullet journal lol. plus the ServSafe cert could help me get supervisor way sooner
do it. also i can show you around campus if you want. i know where the good vending machines are which is honestly the most important info
lmaooo you're ridiculous. but yeah maybe i'll come check it out this weekend. thanks tony
got you
